key_insight: The mean inner potential of alloys and strained materials can be predicted by combining a linear composition term at constant volume with a volume-rescaling correction.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical way to estimate electrostatic potential changes in semiconductor alloys and strained structures, supporting electron microscopy, device modeling, and materials design.
method: DFT calculations with an augmented plane waves plus local orbitals basis set
Compare MIP trends for alloy composition at constant volume versus changing volume
Use volume-dependent rescaling to capture bowing in mixed systems and strain effects
evidence_signal: The abstract reports clear linear MIP-composition behavior for AlGaAs and bowing for InGaP and GeSi, which is explained by including volume rescaling.
watch_item: The model accuracy depends on how well DFT and the rescaling rule represent real alloy disorder, strain states, and non-ideal structural effects.
paper_title: Modelling the mean inner potential of alloyed and strained materials
